description: One incident triggers dozens of separate pages because alerts lack grouping and inhibition.
body: Failure mode: a single underlying incident fires many correlated alerts with no grouping or inhibition, burying the on-call in pages and obscuring the root cause. Detection signals: dozens of pages within seconds for one outage; duplicate alerts for the same incident; on-call dismissing pages without reading them. Do not send every alert ungrouped. Safer fallback: group alerts by shared labels, add inhibition so the top-level failure suppresses dependents, and demote non-actionable alerts off the paging path.
